title: CA's IT Resource Management Solutions Provide a Basis for Running IT Operations as a Business Entity
author: Valerie O'Connell
source_file: 2001 CA_s IT Resource Management Solutions P...ing IT Operations as a Business Entity.pdf
abstract: This Aberdeen Impact examines Computer Associates' IT Resource Management (ITRM) solution set, which provides an integrated but modular platform for managing IT as a cohesive business entity. The study argues that running IT as a unified business — sharing information and automating processes across functions — requires bridging diversity of objectives across IT operations, Help Desk, and Accounting. Author Valerie O'Connell positions CA ITRM as the solution to this organizational challenge.
importance_rationale: Captures an early articulation of IT service management as a business discipline; CA was a major IT management software vendor in 2001 and ITRM was a significant product suite in the enterprise systems management market.
relevance_rationale: The specific CA ITRM product suite has been superseded; CA was acquired by Broadcom in 2018 and many products discontinued. However, the IT-as-a-business-entity framework anticipated ITIL/ITSM concepts that remain current.
prescience_rationale: The prediction that unified IT management platforms would be needed proved correct; however, CA was ultimately acquired by Broadcom (2018) rather than becoming the dominant platform, and ITRM was not the lasting solution as predicted.
